---
pagetitle: "2026 World Cup Forecast | Above .500"
---

```{python}
#| output: asis
from above500 import render
from above500.soccer import wc_spi
from html import escape

f = wc_spi.forecast()

print(f'<span class="kicker">{escape(f["league"])}</span>')
print(f'<h1 class="headline">{escape(f["name"])}</h1>')
print(f'<p class="dek">{escape(f["description"])}</p>')
print(render.byline(f))
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
columns = [
    {"key": "name", "label": "Team", "kind": "team"},
    {"key": "spi", "label": "SPI", "kind": "num"},
    {"key": "attack", "label": "Off", "kind": "dec", "hide_sm": True},
    {"key": "defense", "label": "Def", "kind": "dec", "hide_sm": True},
    {"key": "group", "label": "Group", "kind": "text"},
    {"key": "history", "label": "Since Jun '24", "kind": "spark", "hide_sm": True},
    {"key": "r32_prob", "label": "Make round of 32", "kind": "prob"},
    {"key": "qf_prob", "label": "Make quarters", "kind": "prob"},
    {"key": "sf_prob", "label": "Make semis", "kind": "prob"},
    {"key": "title_prob", "label": "Win World Cup", "kind": "prob"},
]
print(render.odds_table(f["standings"], columns,
                        "Who will win the World Cup?",
                        f"48 teams — through {f['data_through']}",
                        sort_key="title_prob"))
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print(render.fixtures_table(f["upcoming"], "Upcoming matches",
                            "Win/draw/win probabilities"))
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
print('<details class="model-performance">')
print('<summary>Model performance &amp; backtest</summary>')
print(render.backtest_models_table(f["backtest"]))
print(render.calibration_table(f["backtest"]))
if f.get("wc_backtest"):
    print(render.wc_backtest_table(f["wc_backtest"]))
print('</details>')
print(render.methodology_box(f))
```
